nodes.name: 紅臉地犀鳥
nodes.summary: 紅臉地犀鳥（Southern ground hornbill，学名 Bucorvus leadbeateri），在图谱中标记为易危（VU）物种，常见栖息地包括稀树草原、峡谷峭壁，主要威胁包括栖息地破坏、猎捕压力、森林砍伐。
nodes.shortSummary: 紅臉地犀鳥（學名：Bucorvus leadbeateri）是地犀鳥科地犀鳥屬下的鳥類。牠們分佈於東部及南部非洲上超過700萬平方公里的較潮溼草原及林地內。紅臉地犀鳥多以地面上的動物為食，包括節肢動物、青蛙、爬蟲類、囓齒動物和小型脊椎動物等。 牠們體型巨大，是犀鳥目內最重的物種…

nodes.name: 鸟纲
nodes.summary: 鸟纲（Aves）是图谱中的分类级分类节点，当前关联10767种鸟类，如丹顶鹤、朱鹮、中华秋沙鸭等10767种鸟类。

nodes.name: 栖息地破坏
nodes.summary: 开发、采伐和围垦等活动导致适宜生境减少。

nodes.name: 紅臉地犀鳥
nodes.summary: 紅臉地犀鳥（Bucorvus leadbeateri）是图谱中的分类级分类节点，当前关联1种鸟类，如紅臉地犀鳥。

nodes.name: 峡谷峭壁
nodes.summary: 大型猛禽和食腐鸟偏好的高崖栖息环境。

nodes.name: 森林砍伐
nodes.summary: 原生林被砍伐导致栖息地面积和连通性下降。

nodes.name: 猎捕压力
nodes.summary: 直接猎捕或误捕造成额外死亡。

nodes.name: 脊索动物门
nodes.summary: 脊索动物门（Chordata）是图谱中的分类级分类节点，当前关联10767种鸟类，如丹顶鹤、朱鹮、中华秋沙鸭等10767种鸟类。

nodes.name: 动物界
nodes.summary: 动物界（Animalia）是图谱中的分类级分类节点，当前关联10767种鸟类，如丹顶鹤、朱鹮、中华秋沙鸭等10767种鸟类。

nodes.name: VU
nodes.summary: VU表示 IUCN 易危等级，当前关联694种鸟类，如丹顶鹤、索马里鸵鸟、大斑几维鸟等694种鸟类。

nodes.name: Bucorvus
nodes.summary: Bucorvus（Bucorvus）是图谱中的分类级分类节点，当前关联2种鸟类，如地犀鸟、紅臉地犀鳥。

nodes.name: 稀树草原
nodes.summary: 东非常见的开放草地与湿地边缘复合景观。

links.label: 栖息于
links.evidence: It can be found in the southern regions of Africa, ranging from Kenya to South Africa. Within these regions, they inhabit both woodlands and savannas. The other species of the genus Bucorvus is the Abyssinian ground hornbill, B

links.label: 栖息于
links.evidence: Their food ranges from insects to small vertebrates. Their nests are often found in high tree cavities or other shallow cavities, such as rock holes in cliff faces. These birds are a long-lived species, having lifespans in the range of 50–60 years, and up to 70 in captivity. In relation to their long lives, they do not reach sexual maturity until 4–6 years old, and begin breeding around 10 years old. Their sex can be identified by the colour of their throats: the male's is pure red and the female's is a deep violet-blue. Southern ground hornbills are a culturally pervasive and important species in southern Africa

links.label: 受威胁于
links.evidence: Habitat loss, changes due to the agriculture, deforestation, electrocution from power lines, accidental poisoning, and persecution are the major factors that affect their populations. Persecution and hunting of the southern ground hornbill by human populations have continued to be complex issues
